rationale: Khan and Dhar (2006) is cited in the Study 1 discussion when the authors consider and dismiss a licensing-based "license to sin" account as the main explanation for their findings. This is more than a generic background mention because it is used to interpret results and differentiate mechanisms. At the same time, the citing paper does not directly build on or extend the cited paper's framework or methods. So the citation is substantive, but its function is best treated as a contrastive conceptual role.
relation_description: The citing paper invokes the licensing effect literature (including Khan and Dhar 2006) as an alternative mechanism and argues that this account is less applicable to its observed effects, favoring an attention-to-quantity explanation instead. The cited work is used mainly for conceptual contrast in interpretation.
